title: Except One
description:
You are given three integers $p$, $k$, $t$, where $p$ is a prime number.

The set $S$ is defined as follows: $S = \{x \mid x \in N, \ 1 \leq x \leq p - 1, \ x \neq k \}$.

Find the sum of the products of all $t$-element subsets of $S$, modulo $p$.
input: The first line contains three integers $p$, $k$, $t$ ($p$ is a prime number; $1 \leq k \leq p-1$; $1 \leq t \leq p-2$; $p \leq 10^9$).
output: Print the sum of the products of all $t$-element subsets of $S$, modulo $p$.
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Summer 2023, Day 6: olmrgcsi And His Friends’ Contest
